stem: The length of one side of square X is 4 times the length of one side of square Y. The area of square Y is 25 square feet. What is the area, in square feet, of square X?
rationale: Choice B is correct. The area of a square with side length s is s2. Square Y has area 25, so its side length is √25 = 5 feet. Square X has side length 4(5) = 20 feet, so its area is 202 = 400 square feet. Choice A is the area of a square with side length 4 times the side length of Y divided by 2. Choice C corresponds to multiplying the area of Y by 8. Choice D is the side length of X.
